UAE Oil Production Nears Record High After OPEC Exit
headlines.summary: The UAE is wasting little time capitalizing on its exit from OPEC, with crude production already climbing above 3.8 million barrels per day in June, its highest level in more than six years, as Abu Dhabi rapidly converts spare capacity into exports despite weaker oil prices. headlines.title: Russia’s Oil Windfall Vanishes as Urals Crashes to $42 a Barrel
headlines.summary: Russian crude oil prices have fallen back to where they were before the Middle East war, with its flagship Urals averaging just $41.66 a barrel during the first three days of July. headlines.title: Fear of an Oil Glut May Be Overblown, Top Consultancy Says
headlines.summary: Up to 75% of the previous oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz are expected to return to the market by the end of the year, but significantly lower oil prices aren’t guaranteed for 2027 as the ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions are unlikely to be resolved for good soon, Fereidun Feshar

headlines.title: Germany Weathers Energy Crisis With Growing Dependence on American LNG
headlines.summary: The share of LNG imports of Germany’s total gas supply rose to 12% in the first half of the year, up from 10% a year earlier, despite the shock supply loss from the Middle East due to the closed Strait of Hormuz, according to data by the German regulator.
